question.text: As per SEBI mandate, all stock derivatives in India are being moved to which type of settlement?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI has mandated physical settlement (delivery of underlying stock) for all stock derivatives, moving away from cash settlement. This transition is being done in a phased manner as per SEBI circulars. Physical settlement requires actual delivery of shares on exercise/expiry. **The Trap:** Cash settlement was the earlier method — the shift to physical settlement is the current SEBI mandate. **Cross-Exam Context:** Settlement mechanisms for derivatives are tested in VIII, X-A, and VII (Securities Operations). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Physical settlement means clients must have shares in demat account (for short positions) or funds to take delivery (for long positions) — ensure clients don't accidentally go to expiry without preparation.
